LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS MARK BROWN AND DEIRDRE BROWN'S MOTION FOR ORDER IN AID OF APPRAISAL REFERENCE (CORP CODE 17351) IS GRANTED. THE COURT ADOPTS PLAINTIFFS' METHOD FOR THE SELECTION OF THE 3 APPRAISERS. THE COURT ADOPTS AUGUST 18, 2008 AS THE VALUATION DATE. THE PARTIES MAY AGREE TO AN ALTERNATE DATE. THE PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER TO SET A DISCOVERY SCHEDULE AND/OR AGREE TO AN EXCHANGE OF DOCUMENTS AND DEPOSITIONS IF NECESSARY. THE PARTIES ARE ALSO TO AGREE ON A SCHEDULE FOR BRIEFING AND HEARING THE LEASE ISSUES AND VALUATION METHOD, IF NECESSARY. EACH SIDE TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT AN ORDER FOR THE COURT'S CONSIDERATION. Notice Of Motion For Order In Aid Of Appraisal Reference (Corp Code 17351)
Off Calendar Aug-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
J. Brian McCauley, Esq. (415-974-1515) appearing for plaintiffs /moving party Mark Brown
John F. Granucci, Esq. of O'DONNELL, WAISS (415-362-6068), appearing for defendants Felix Contreras, Robert McCartney, Antique Enterprises, LLC
Christopher H. Hart, Esq. of SCHNADER, HARRISON (415-364-6700), appearing for Antiques for 7

Ruling:
PLAINTIFFS MARK BROWN & DEIRDRE BROWN's Motion For Order In Aid Of Appraisal Reference (Corp Code 17351)
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFS MARK BROWN AND DEIRDRE BROWN'S Motion For Order In Aid Of Appraisal Reference (Corp Code 17351). The motion is granted. The Court adopts plaintiffs' method for the selection of the 3 appraisers. The Court adopts August 18, 2008 as the valuation date. The parties may agree to an alternate date. The parties are to meet and confer to set a discovery schedule and/or agree to an exchange of documents and depositions if necessary. The parties are also to agree on a schedule for briefing and hearing the lease issues and valuation method, if necessary. Each side to prepare and submit an order for the Court's consideration. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/PB)

APPEARANCES: DAVID S. SEMEL, ESQUIRE (COLOMBATTO SIMS, 415.391.6182) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JOHN F. GRANUCCI, ESQUIRE (O'DONNELL WAISS WALL & MESCHKE, 415.362.6068) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS. CHRISTOPHER H. HART, ESQUIRE (SCHNADER HARRISON SEGAL & LEWIS, 415.364.6700) SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR INTERESTED PARTY (ANTIQUES FOR SEVEN).
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. UNDER CORPORATIONS CODE SECTION 17351(B)(2) THE CLERK SHOULD NOT HAVE DISMISSED THE ACTION AFTER DEFENDANTS' EXERCISED THEIR RIGHT TO PURCHASE PLAINTIFF'S INTEREST. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
